title: Schaum's Outline: Data Structures Hierarchy
description: A hierarchical diagram showing the classification of fundamental data structures covered in Schaum's Outlines, grouped by their logical structure and memory allocation type.
elements: Linear Data Structures (Arrays, Linked Lists, Stacks, Queues)
Dynamic Memory Allocation (Linked Lists, Trees, Graphs)
relationships: Primitive structures form the building blocks for all other data structures
Linear structures organize data sequentially, while Non-Linear structures organize hierarchically or interconnectedly
Static allocation provides fixed size, Dynamic allocation allows flexible size
Homogeneous structures store same data type, Non-Homogeneous store different data types
